title: Built a scanner to detect unusual options flow -here's what it's showing today
author: BuildwithPublic
selftext:
Been working on a flow scanner that flags institutional positioning signals before price moves. Sharing today's output because it's pretty interesting.

The criteria it uses:

* Volume > 5x open interest = fresh positioning (not rolling)
* OTM puts hitting the ask in size = someone buying directional protection
* High V/OI with zero prior OI = brand new position opened today

**What it's showing right now across SPY/QQQ/NVDA/TSLA/AAPL:**

* 140 bearish signals vs 54 bullish
* $118.7M top notional flow
* SPY $631 PUT (Apr 24) — 1,019x V/OI. Opened from nothing today.
* QQQ $578 PUT (Apr 17) — 85x V/OI, $1.1M notional, ask-side

Not saying it's definitely smart money. But size + urgency + context = signal. Everything else is noise.

Built on the r/PublicApp  API with their OpenClaw integration-  pulls live chains every time I run it.

Happy to answer questions on the methodology.

https://preview.redd.it/u0v05zrzagog1.png?width=1549&format=png&auto=webp&s=fa9267ff14ff22138e4978dd7ca00270a89ce728

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
Interesting breakdown. A couple questions on your methodology:

1) How are you filtering out hedge-related flow (e.g., dealer gamma hedging or fund-level portfolio protection) vs actual directional bets? With indices especially, heavy OTM put buying can just be systematic downside hedging rather than a near-term bearish signal.

2) Are you weighting by DTE? A 5x V/OI spike in 0DTE contracts means something very different from the same signal 60–90 days out. I’ve found short-dated OTM put sweeps can exaggerate “bearish” counts without translating into sustained downside.

3) On the 140 vs 54 split — is that raw contract count, notional-weighted, or ticker-weighted? $118.7M top notional sounds meaningful, but across SPY/QQQ/NVDA/TSLA/AAPL that’s not massive unless it’s concentrated in one expiry/strike cluster.

It’d be interesting to see:
- Net premium (paid vs received)
- Call skew changes intraday
- Whether these signals cluster around key levels (e.g., NVDA near prior highs)

Cool project overall — unusual flow is useful context, but the edge usually comes from distinguishing speculative flow from systematic hedging. If you can separate those, this could be powerful.

comments.author: AbilityBoring2173
comments.body:
Hey! Was it difficult to write this code?
Ive been wanting to do exactly this for myself but I have zero experience coding. Is it possible using an llm?
